title: Just Another Day: How to Survive an Active Shooter Event on Campus
body: Reasons to Not be a School Shooter: The kids are gonna hit you with those big science and social studies textbooks. The hard-cover ones.
author: @thundertoon1398

title: Just Another Day: How to Survive an Active Shooter Event on Campus
body:
"Although it's highly unlikely you'll ever encounter a school shooting..."
Tell that the every kid in America who hears about a new shooting every week
author: @xi5450

title: Just Another Day: How to Survive an Active Shooter Event on Campus
body: Last year my school had a shooting threat. My teacher sent me to the library so I was away from the rest of my class. When I was going to leave the library, a teacher told me I needed to stay and the doors were locked. Nobody explained what was going on, but I was horrified. It felt like my worst nightmare was about to come true. For over 40 minutes I sat in the library. I heard some of the adults saying “how are we going to hide this many kids?!” To one another. After 40 minutes, someone got on the intercoms and said we could end the “drill” and she sounded so scared and that will stay with me forever I think. We later got an email about the threat made. It was terrifying and I dint know if that would be the day I’d have to be in a school shooting and I’m so glad that it was just a threat. I still get panicked when I hear the intercoms in my school because I’m afraid of hearing that were going into a lockdown.
author: @HankHill000

title: Just Another Day: How to Survive an Active Shooter Event on Campus
body: Not to be that guy, but in that after-scene, those students are so calm. Anybody at my school would've been crying, having a panic attack, but we're all idiots, so most of all, tons of us would die.
author: @The-Yappatron-Is-Real

title: Just Another Day: How to Survive an Active Shooter Event on Campus
body: The fact that this is the reality these days is insane. It’s so good to see that Towson isn’t being ignorant to the fact that this is happening though and they’re actually educating their students on what to do in a situation like this.
author: @LukeHutch

title: Freshman College Move In Vlog 2017 // Towson University
body:
Hey Lindsay, i'm also attending Towson this fall as a freshman. How did you get that "social media on campus job" even before you started school. I know about handshake but i dont really see towson owned jobs on there.
Can you please enlighten me more.
Also to be frank, do you think i'll be at disadvantage on handshake because i've never worked a job?
Thanks.
author: @TeeTee-lc3vy

title: Freshman College Move In Vlog 2017 // Towson University
body: The reason why I was searching for move in day videos was to know how anyone would carry so much of stuff to college. But all of you guys move in with a car. But this august I need to travel 3500 miles to go to college. I'll must be travelling in a flight. How in the world would I carry so much of stuff in a flight? Flights have luggage weight limits. Asking my family to mail me would be as expensive as asking them to carry the stuff by a second trip due to import and export duties. I wanna save some bucks here as I am on a budget. Please get me some ideas on how you guys would have done it if you were in the same situation.
author: @justinzcruiz9808
